P·WHIFF: Green=above LG avg (pitcher advantage) · Red=below LG avg. MATCH = pitcher whiff × (opp÷LG) — how well this pitch works against this lineup
K%/Whiff% = batter actual rate vs this pitcher's exact pitch mix, weighted by usage. Green = pitcher advantage · Red = pitcher disadvantage. Coverage dot: ●high ●mod ●low ●no data
Signal
Play the over 4.5.2 units
High-confidence edge — 76% model conviction at 7.0K vs 4.5 line.
Miller is above average on whiff rate (28.4%) — solid enough to capitalize when lineup cooperates. SL is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. PIT is a good matchup — 25.46% MIX-K above LG, lineup vulnerability is a real tailwind. He's in form — recent trend is a tailwind.
Bet wins if
✓SL converts two-strike counts (34% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Sharp command (1.1 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
✓PIT swings vs his specific mix (25.5% MIX-K) — lineup is genuinely exploitable
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 5.8 IP — short outings cap K upside hard

Signal
Play the over 4.5.2 units
High-confidence edge — 72% model conviction at 6.6K vs 4.5 line.
Boyd generates genuine swing-and-miss (31.2% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. SL is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. NYM is roughly neutral — 22.92% MIX-K close to league average. Moderate variance (σ2.1K) — no major consistency flags.
Bet wins if
✓SL converts two-strike counts (37% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Elite swing-and-miss stuff (31.2% WHF) — Ks can stack quickly when he's on
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 4.8 IP — short outings cap K upside hard

Signal
Play the over 5.5.2 units
High-confidence edge — 68% model conviction at 7.3K vs 5.5 line.
Sánchez generates genuine swing-and-miss (31.4% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. SL is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. WSH is tougher than it looks — MIX-K% drops to 19.82%, below league average vs his specific pitch mix. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.
Bet wins if
✓He logs his average 7.1 IP — enough PA to let Ks accumulate
✓SL converts two-strike counts (37% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Sharp command (1.7 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
Bet loses if
✗WSH makes contact vs his pitch mix (19.8% MIX-K) — tough K environment today
✗High variance (σ2.7K) — his floor is well below projection

Signal
Over 5.5 is the play.1 unit
Solid over edge at 6.7K vs 5.5. 62% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Schlittler is above average on whiff rate (27.9%) — solid enough to capitalize when lineup cooperates. CU is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. BOS is a mild headwind — 21.82% MIX-K just below average vs his specific stuff. He's in form — recent trend is a tailwind.
Bet wins if
✓CU converts two-strike counts (33% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Sharp command (1.7 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 5.9 IP — short outings cap K upside hard
✗High variance (σ2.7K) — his floor is well below projection

Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
Solid over edge at 5.4K vs 4.5. 61% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Early sits around league average on whiff rate (23.0%) — relies more on weak contact than pure swing-and-miss. SW is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. NYY is roughly neutral — 24.2% MIX-K close to league average. Moderate variance (σ1.6K) — no major consistency flags. Edge lives at the 4.5 alt line, not the main 5.5 — be on the right number.
Bet wins if
✓SW converts two-strike counts (27% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 5.7 IP — short outings cap K upside hard

Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
Solid over edge at 5.3K vs 4.5. 58% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Imai generates genuine swing-and-miss (32.0% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. SI is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. DET is roughly neutral — 23.54% MIX-K close to league average. He's in form — recent trend is a tailwind.
Bet wins if
✓SI converts two-strike counts (38% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Elite swing-and-miss stuff (32.0% WHF) — Ks can stack quickly when he's on
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 4.1 IP — short outings cap K upside hard
✗Control is a problem (5.3 BB/9) — walks eat PA and he works from behind in counts
✗First-pitch strike rate (50%) is low — he'll work from behind and generate weak contact, not Ks

P·WHIFF: Green=above LG avg (pitcher advantage) · Red=below LG avg. MATCH = pitcher whiff × (opp÷LG) — how well this pitch works against this lineup
K%/Whiff% = batter actual rate vs this pitcher's exact pitch mix, weighted by usage. Green = pitcher advantage · Red = pitcher disadvantage. Coverage dot: ●high ●mod ●low ●no data
Signal
Lean under 4.5 — size down.0.5 unit
Under edge present (3.0K vs 4.5), 23% confidence — lean only.
Lugo is below average on swing-and-miss (17.9% WHF vs 25% LG) — needs favorable conditions to reach Ks. Sl is a serviceable finishing pitch, not dominant in two-strike counts. TB is a mild headwind — 20.52% MIX-K just below average vs his specific stuff. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence. Edge lives at the 4.5 alt line, not the main 3.5 — be on the right number.
Bet wins if
✓Low strikeout rate (K/9 7.0) — he misses bats infrequently, line is inflated
✓Elite first-pitch strike rate (69%) — batters put the ball in play early, not deep counts
✓Short average outing (5.4 IP) — fewer PA limits total K ceiling
Bet loses if
✗K/9 of 7.0 means enough talent to reach 4.5 in a deep outing

Model projects 4.6K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Gore sits around league average on whiff rate (25.7%) — relies more on weak contact than pure swing-and-miss. SI is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. TOR is tougher than it looks — MIX-K% drops to 19.98%, below league average vs his specific pitch mix. He's in form — recent trend is a tailwind.

Model v9.5
K Props · F5 · NRFI · methodology + weightings · v9.5 edge% tiering
⚡ K Props — Strikeout Projection v9.2
L10-based, 7-factor formula. Each L10 start normalized by opponent K rate: adj_k = raw_k × (22.7 / opp_K%). Base = adj_L10_avg × 0.70 + k9_proj × 0.30. K/9 talent is embedded in base — no separate term, no double-count. All 7 factors are ratios vs league average × base × weight.
Adj L10 base · opp-normalized · 70% L10 + 30% K/9 in base
32%
Whiff% vs lg avg (25%) — strongest Statcast K predictor
22%
Opp K% vs lg avg (22.7%) — today's matchup context
18%
Arsenal quality · ELITE 1.18 · STRONG 1.09 · MOD 1.00 · WEAK 0.88
11%
Putaway% vs lg avg (19%) — 2-strike whiff rate
9%
FPS% vs lg avg (63%) — command drives deep counts
6%
Park factor inverted (2.0 − park) — small but real
2%
Strong ≥1.5K edge · Moderate 0.5–1.4K · Under ≤−1.2K · Pass <0.5K edge
🔴 F5 — First 5 Innings Run Projection
ERA base blended 60% pitcher ERA + 40% league average (4.3R F5). Prevents extreme suppression from elite starters. wRC+ is hand-adjusted (vs RHP or LHP). L10 R/G captures recent offensive form. WHIP captures baserunner risk beyond strikeouts. IP capped at 5.0 for F5.
L5 F5 ERA base × IP × park adj
35%
Lineup wRC+ vs pitcher handedness (lg avg 100)
25%
Opponent L10 R/G vs lg avg (4.35)
20%
FPS% vs lg avg (63%) — command = fewer baserunners
12%
WHIP vs lg avg (1.20) — baserunner rate
8%
📋 Full Game — Complete Game Run Projection
ERA base blended 60% pitcher ERA + 40% league average (8.7R full game). SP and bullpen each weighted separately. wRC+ hand-adjusted. Park factor applied to both teams. L10 R/G for offensive form. FG uses 5.5 IP default vs 5.0 for F5.
Starter ERA vs lg avg (4.50) × proj innings
30%
Bullpen ERA vs lg avg (4.20) × remaining innings
25%
Lineup wRC+ vs pitcher handedness (lg avg 100)
20%
Park factor — direct multiplier on run environment
15%
Opponent L10 R/G vs lg avg (4.35)
10%
🎯 NRFI / YRFI — First Inning Scoring
Poisson model. Expected runs per inning = (opp L10 R/G ÷ 9) adjusted by ERA, FPS%, and WHIP. P(0 runs) = e^(−exp_r) per team. Combined NRFI = away_prob × home_prob. Higher FPS + lower ERA + tougher lineup = closer to NRFI.
SP ERA vs lg avg (4.50) — primary scoring suppressor
~35%
FPS% vs lg avg (63%) — command = fewer baserunners in 1st
~30%
WHIP vs lg avg (1.20) — baserunner risk
~20%
Opponent L10 R/G ÷ 9 — per-inning run environment
~15%
NRFI ≥62% · NRFI lean 57–61% · Skip 43–56% · YRFI ≤42%
Top 4 lineup OBP shown with individual player names. Danger ≥.350 avg OBP · Watch ≥.330 · Manageable <.330
📐 Implied Odds Box
Poisson distribution (λ = proj K) implied odds for 4 alt line thresholds. O% + U% = 100%. Bold row = closest to the FanDuel line. Compare to FanDuel alt lines to find additional edge.
P(K ≥ n) = 1 − Σ(k=0 to n−1) [e^−λ × λ^k / k!]
American odds: P≥50% → −round(P/(1−P)×100)
📡 Data Sources
✓ Real (API) — L10 K counts · pitch counts · season ERA/K9/BB9/WHIP · FanDuel K prop lines + odds
⚠ Researched — Arsenal whiff% · FPS% · putaway% · matchup K rates · park factors · bullpen ERA · L5 F5 ERA · lineup OBP + wRC+ · top 4 threats
✗ Gaps — L10 opponents (API returns ? — normalization falls back to raw avg) · live Statcast splits · real-time lineup changes
↑
